description_long: This object is a medieval-style sword with a straight, double-edged blade and a simple, robust design. The blade is long and tapers slightly towards the tip, with a uniform dark coloration suggesting a metal construction, possibly steel. The crossguard is straight and unadorned, providing basic hand protection, while the handle is cylindrical and ends in a rounded pommel, which appears solid and functional. The entire sword has a matte, non-reflective finish, giving it a utilitarian appearance with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The proportions and style are reminiscent of European arming swords, suitable for one-handed use. The object is visually consistent with practical weaponry rather than ceremonial or decorative swords.
description: A straight, double-edged medieval-style sword with a simple crossguard and rounded pommel, finished in a matte dark color. The design is utilitarian and unadorned.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, handle, and rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the same features visible.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, showing the blade's thin profile and the end of the pommel.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, solid, rounded pommel, no visible wrapping or decoration
